title: conyza incana
intro: Conyza incana, also known as fleabane or horseweed, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat bacterial infections. The health benefits of Conyza incana include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and bronchodilatory properties. Conyza incana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal capsules and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
